placement.decision: Replace Runtime.shutdown with an idempotent Runtime.deactivate method and register that method through the existing session resource cleanup closure.
placement.reason: The runtime owns active attempts, cancellation controllers, finish arbitration, and the cached lifecycle promises. Existing Lite cleanup already supplies ordered async settlement and error propagation.

state_boundary.durable_state: Only a record accepted by store.commit is durable session business state.
state_boundary.activation_state: The Runtime record is an activation-local working copy until an explicit checkpoint or finish commits a snapshot.
allowed_deactivation_changes: Move the private activation lifecycle from active to deactivating to deactivated
Abort the activation's live attempt controllers
Record cancelled attempt and work settlement in the activation-local working copy
forbidden_deactivation_changes: Change SessionRecord.status or SessionRecord.version when explicit finish did not already win
Admit new work after deactivation linearizes

deactivation_semantics.linearization_point: The first deactivate call synchronously changes the private activation lifecycle from active to deactivating before aborting work.
deactivation_semantics.cancellation: Snapshot every active attempt at the linearization point, abort every controller in admission order with DOMException name AbortError and message Session deactivated, then admit no new work.
deactivation_semantics.join: Await settlement of every attempt in that snapshot. Abort all first; do not abort and await serially. An already settled attempt is absent from the snapshot and is untouched.
deactivation_semantics.idempotency: Cache and return one deactivation promise. Repeated calls do not abort, settle, commit, or reorder cleanup again.
deactivation_semantics.normal_result: Cancelled is an AttemptSettlement value, not a cleanup error.
deactivation_semantics.settlement_error: If an attempt settlement promise rejects, still await every snapshotted attempt, then reject deactivation with the one error or an AggregateError for multiple errors. Never commit.
deactivation_semantics.cleanup_order: Use existing Lite order: execution onClose callbacks run first in LIFO order; resources then clean up in reverse resolution order; each resource cleanup is LIFO and awaited. Session deactivation occupies its resource cleanup slot and resolves only after all active attempts join.
deactivation_semantics.lite_close_error_policy: On a successful context close, Lite propagates resource cleanup failures after running the remaining cleanups. On a failed context close, Lite runs resource cleanups safely and does not replace the original failure. SDK deactivation does not hide its own settlement failure.

explicit_persistence_semantics.checkpoint_before_close: An explicit checkpoint may commit an open snapshot. Later deactivation makes no second commit and leaves the stored status and version exactly as checkpoint produced them.
explicit_persistence_semantics.finish_before_close: Explicit finish owns cancellation, join, and one finished commit. Later deactivation awaits the cached finish completion and makes no second commit.
explicit_persistence_semantics.finish_wins_race: If finish synchronously claims its cached completion before deactivate linearizes, finish commits once. Deactivate awaits that same explicit completion.
explicit_persistence_semantics.deactivate_wins_race: If deactivate changes the activation lifecycle first, a later finish call rejects as deactivating or deactivated and makes no commit.

executable_cases.expected: zero aborts, zero commits, durable and runtime status/version unchanged
abort once, wait until cancelled settlement, zero commits, durable status/version unchanged
abort all before waiting, resolve only after every sibling settles, zero commits
no new abort or settlement and zero commits
one explicit open checkpoint commit and no cleanup commit or persisted change
one explicit finished commit and no cleanup commit
same cached promise and one cancellation/join sequence
one finished commit; deactivation awaits explicit finish
finish rejects and commit count remains zero
all attempts join, deactivation rejects, commit count remains zero

progression_test_contract: Exercise deactivation through createScope, the public session resource, and ExecutionContext.close.
Assert the ten executable cases and exact commit counts.
Assert the durable record object and store status/version before and after ordinary close.
Assert activation-local work and attempts settle cancelled without treating that working copy as durable.
Assert AbortError reason, abort-all-before-wait, join-all, cached-promise idempotency, and post-linearization admission rejection.
Assert successful-close error propagation, failed-close original-error preservation, and resource cleanup order.
Keep the verified current-owned activation behavior at 6 of 6.
Do not add a Lite primitive, nearest-context ownership, shared scope factory, hidden cleanup effect, start, or spawn API.
decision: candidate only; not accepted by this worker
